ops.text: Scope-diverged plugin installs FIXED (user directive: don't tolerate the drift the scope-precedence dedupe papers over). cc-orchestrate project scope (projectPath=~/Code/cc-orchestrate) updated 7da2dcb10710 → 88ae1067b41e = origin/main HEAD, now identical to user scope; the same sweep surfaced captain-hook@captain-hook project scope (~/Code/captain-hook) stale at 10.5.0 vs user 10.6.0 — updated. Final roster sweep: zero version-diverged duplicate ids. ROOT CAUSE + recurring trap: `claude plugin update` defaults to --scope user even when run inside a project, so project-scope enablements never ride routine updates and drift stale — and project scope WINS, so the repo runs old code. v11 rollout checklist addition (Phase 4): after tagging v11.0.0, run `claude plugin update captain-hook@captain-hook --scope project` in every repo with a project-scope captain-hook enablement (currently just ~/Code/captain-hook itself), or dispatch there runs v10.
